# Schedule Results Checklist: UC-23 Request Multiple Schedule Results

**Purpose**: Validate the completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurability of the UC-23 multi-result scheduling requirements before implementation review.
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/thaia/ECE493Group19/specs/023-branch-schedule-results/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the required student inputs fully specified, including selected course set, scheduling term, and requested result count? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §Dependencies]
- [x] CHK002 Are the required result-state outcomes fully documented for ranked success, capped requests, reduced results, partial timeout, timeout failure, invalid input, and blocked preconditions? [Completeness, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-010, Spec §NFR-004]
- [x] CHK003 Are the minimum comparison details for each ranked schedule option explicitly defined if the student is expected to compare alternatives? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK004 Are the dependency requirements for authentication, term availability, and catalog/timetable availability explicitly documented and bounded? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Dependencies]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is the phrase “distinct schedule options” defined precisely enough to tell whether only section combinations must differ or whether timing/layout differences are also required? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK006 Is the “consistent ranking policy” described clearly enough that reviewers can tell whether repeated requests with unchanged inputs must return the same order? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK007 Is the timeout/compute-limit condition bounded clearly enough for reviewers to distinguish timeout behavior from ordinary reduced-result behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-008, Spec §NFR-004]
- [x] CHK008 Is the fixed 1 to 10 request-count range stated consistently and unambiguously across stories, requirements, assumptions, and success criteria? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-001, Spec §Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Are capping behavior and reduced-result behavior kept distinct and consistent between user stories, edge cases, and functional requirements? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK010 Do timeout scenarios remain consistent between the user stories, edge cases, and requirements for partial results versus no-result failure? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK011 Do planning-only requirements align consistently across user stories, functional requirements, and measurable outcomes? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-011, Spec §SC-001]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK012 Are all success criteria objectively measurable without relying on implementation-specific ranking algorithms or internal compute metrics? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK013 Do the acceptance scenarios define observable outcomes strongly enough to distinguish capped success, reduced success, partial timeout, and timeout failure? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK014 Can the requirement for “appropriate recovery guidance” be objectively verified from the current wording, or does it need more precise guidance content? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §SC-006, Spec §NFR-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Are primary, alternate, and exception flows all represented in the written requirements for requesting multiple results, handling capped counts, and recovering from failures? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK016 Are requirements defined for the boundary request counts of exactly 1 and exactly 10 results? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK017 Are requirements specified for blank input, non-numeric input, timeout-after-partial-results, timeout-before-results, and blocked precondition scenarios? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK018 Is the performance target tied to a concrete user-visible event for the multi-result flow? [Measurability, Spec §NFR-002, Spec §SC-007, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK019 Are accessibility requirements explicitly specified for the request-count input, ranked result list, and every failure state? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-001]
- [x] CHK020 Are privacy requirements for ranked schedules and student-specific request data explicitly stated in the spec, or only implied by the surrounding project context? [Completeness, Spec §NFR-003]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK021 Are assumptions about preselected course sets, fixed product limit, and planning-only behavior validated against the functional requirements rather than left implicit? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Requirements]
- [x] CHK022 Does the spec define what happens when required timetable data is available but stale, inconsistent, or partially loaded? [Dependency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Dependencies, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- Use this checklist to review the requirements text, not implementation behavior.
